title: Great Ashfield — 385th Bomb Group Memorials (3 sites)
location_description: All Saints Church Great Ashfield IP31 3HQ (primary) + former USAAF Station 155 airfield gate
coordinates_approx: 52.271972N / 0.923361E (church confirmed); TM 00584 66203 (airfield gate — needs field verification)
incident_date: 1943-06 to 1945-05 (operational period; 296 missions; 129 aircraft lost; 400+ killed)
rationale_for: 1) Community advocate (Mary Blanche) has photos + PDF in hand. 2) THREE physical memorials confirmed: 1944 airfield plaque (IWM WMR-4674), 1945 church altar/reredos (IWM WMR-4673), 1991 stained glass window (IWM WMR-4676). 3) USAAF precedent exists on BMR (Kessingland, Nailstone, Reedham). 4) Active association 385thbga.com (c)2026. 5) All Saints PCC confirmed maintainer. 6) Exceptional source documentation: IWM x3, Historic England, Suffolk Heritage Explorer, americanairmuseum.com, HMDB x2. 7) Glenn Miller performed here at 200-mission milestone — strong local story.
rationale_against: 1) Group/station memorial not a specific crash site — most BMR entries mark single aircraft incidents. 2) Three memorials across two locations — needs editorial decision: one page or three? 3) Losses were over occupied Europe not in UK (but memorial IS in UK). 4) Airfield gate plaque has no confirmed current maintainer or precise GPS.
recommended_decision: ADD — single grouped entry with church as primary pin; scope narrative around memorial + human stories of the men who flew from here
missing_data_needed: GPS/w3w for airfield gate plaque; current condition of airfield plaque; 385th BGA photo permissions; HMDB content (403 blocked); full names roll from IWM/385th BGA; PCC direct contact
decision_notes: Grouped entry approved. Church as primary location pin. Specific BL branch research needed.

title: Burn Village, North Yorkshire
proposed_by: 578 Squadron RAF stone memorial on Main Road, SE 594 285. Dedicated 1991. Public wayside granite stone commemorating all who served 578 Squadron 1944-1945.

title: Burn Village, North Yorkshire (Methodist Chapel)
proposed_by: 578 Squadron memorial panels in Burn Methodist Chapel. 11 engraved stainless steel panels naming 219 airmen who flew from Burn and never returned. Dedicated 1999.

title: Selby Abbey, North Yorkshire
proposed_by: Main 578 Squadron Memorial in the North Choir Aisle of Selby Abbey. Squadron Memorial Book (bound goatskin), Squadron Standard, and other items. Three phases 1995-2007. Major public memorial.

title: Ryhope, Sunderland, Tyne and Wear
proposed_by: Bronze plaque on the village war memorial commemorating Pilot Officer Cyril Joe Barton VC, 578 Squadron. Dedicated 1985. Barton earned the Victoria Cross on the Nuremberg raid 30/31 March 1944.
aircraft_type: 578squadron.org.uk cross-check — Barton VC is only Bomber Command VC with clear local memorial; high historical value

title: Burchett's Green, Maidenhead, Berkshire
proposed_by: Woodland memorial at crash site of 578 Sqn Halifax LK-Q, 18 July 1944. Pinewood pedestal with stainless steel plaque commemorating 6 crew lost without trace. Dedicated 1998.

title: Silverstone, Northamptonshire
proposed_by: Memorial at entrance to Silverstone motor race track on site of fatal crash of 578 Sqn Halifax LK-S, 31 March 1944. Concrete OS obelisk with stainless steel plaque. 7 crew. Dedicated 1998.
aircraft_type: 578squadron.org.uk cross-check vs BMR database — notable location at Silverstone
